pain_point_label: Student Outcome Tracking & Learning Analytics Absent

matches.match_rationale: Formative assessment data trapped in LMS exports, paper scan results, and disparate grade books must be normalized into a unified schema before any analysis is possible. This operator lacks the data engineering capacity to perform this manually across 500+ staff and multiple campuses. AI-assisted ETL mapping can handle heterogeneous LMS exports (Moodle, Canvas, proprietary) and produce a clean, analysis-ready dataset.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A pipeline that ingests raw LMS gradebook exports and structured survey CSVs, maps them to a canonical student-outcome schema, and loads them into a central store — ready for accreditation reporting or further analytics.

matches.match_rationale: A significant portion of formative assessment data sits on paper — handwritten rubrics, printed exam sheets, and physical feedback forms. OCR and form-field extraction can digitize this content at scale, converting it into structured records that feed outcome tracking systems. Without this digitization step, paper-based records are permanently invisible to any analytics layer.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document-scanning pipeline with AI-driven handwriting recognition and rubric field extraction that converts physical assessment records into structured rows in the student outcome database.

matches.match_rationale: Once outcome data is structured, the operator's core question — 'which students are at risk of not meeting industry-ready competencies?' — is a propensity modeling problem. Individual student feature vectors (attendance, assessment scores, engagement signals) can predict likelihood of failing to reach graduate threshold, enabling early intervention before accreditation evidence gaps accumulate.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An at-risk early-warning model that scores each enrolled student weekly on probability of failing to meet graduate competency standards, surfaced to campus coordinators via a dashboard with recommended interventions.

matches.match_rationale: Employer feedback surveys and graduate employment records can be classified by AI into competency categories and readiness tiers, answering 'Are graduates meeting industry-ready competencies?' at scale. This converts sparse, unstructured feedback signals into accreditation-ready evidence tables without requiring an institutional research team to code responses manually.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A classification layer that ingests employer feedback forms and graduate employment surveys and automatically tags each response against the institution's competency framework, producing aggregated outcome reports per cohort and course.

matches.match_rationale: Accreditation bodies require outcome evidence in narrative form — self-assessment reports, continuous improvement plans, and program review documents. With structured outcome data available, LLMs can auto-draft these required narrative sections grounded in the operator's own data, dramatically reducing the time institutional research staff (minimal as they are) spend on compliance documentation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An accreditation report generator that accepts structured outcome metrics and auto-drafts the required narrative sections (e.g., ASQA self-assessment, TEQSA program review), ready for human review and submission.

matches.match_rationale: Staff and campus heads routinely need to answer one-off questions about outcomes — 'What was the pass rate for Certificate III in Hospitality at the Brisbane campus last year?' — but lack a reporting team to query the data warehouse. A RAG system over structured outcome data lets non-technical administrators ask natural-language questions and receive grounded, cited answers directly from the institution's own records.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A natural-language analytics assistant connected to the institution's outcome data store, allowing campus principals and course coordinators to ask ad-hoc questions about graduate performance, pass rates, and employer feedback without SQL skills.

matches.match_rationale: Collecting post-graduation outcome data (job placement surveys, employer feedback forms, further-study enrollment confirmations) is a multi-step process involving email outreach, form submission, reminder sequencing, and data entry — currently done inconsistently or not at all. A task automation agent can orchestrate the full graduate follow-up workflow end-to-end: send, chase, extract responses, and log structured records without manual coordination.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated graduate-tracking agent that runs quarterly, sends personalised employment survey emails to cohort graduates, follows up non-responders, parses replies, and writes structured outcome records to the central database — requiring zero staff effort per cycle.
